Phellodendron amurense is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The bark of Phellodendron amurense has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, potentially aiding in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.

The bioactive constituents of Phellodendron amurense include alkaloids, such as berberine, phellodendrine, and tetrandrine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The bark of the plant is rich in these bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities.

Phellodendron amurense is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared in the form of capsules, tablets, or tinctures. It is also used as a component in various herbal tea blends, due to its potential health benefits and distinctive aroma.
